<!--
Thanks for the contribution! A few things to check before opening the PR:

- Your change passes `npm run build:lib` and `npm run build:docs` locally.
- If you added a component or changed a prop, the docs page in docs/docs/components/ is updated.
- Commit messages follow the Conventional Commits style (feat:, fix:, docs:, refactor:, ci:, ...).
-->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences: what does this change and why. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (no API change)
- [ ] New component
- [ ] New prop / behavior on an existing component
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] Docs only
- [ ] CI / tooling

## Chromium reference

<!--
Required for new components or visual changes.
Link or describe where this lives in Chromium (chrome://settings,
chrome://bookmarks, side panel, etc.). Screenshots welcome.
Leave blank for bug fixes and non-visual changes.
-->

## Screenshots / recordings

<!--
For visual changes — before / after. Light and dark mode both.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] `npm run build:lib` passes
- [ ] `npm run build:docs` passes
- [ ] New/changed components have a docs page with a `tsx live` preview
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode
- [ ] No new runtime dependencies (or I've explained why one is needed)
- [ ] I've read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

## Related issues

<!-- "Closes #123" or "Part of #456" -->
